pillars.name: Electrostatics
pillars.description: The physics of stationary electric charge: Coulomb's law, the electric field and potential, Gauss's law, and the behaviour of conductors and capacitors.
pillars.descriptionCs: Fyzika nehybného elektrického náboje: Coulombův zákon, elektrické pole a potenciál, Gaussův zákon a chování vodičů a kondenzátorů.

blocks.name: Coulomb's Law & Electric Force
blocks.description: The force between point charges is proportional to their product and inversely proportional to the square of their separation, directed along the line joining them; this inverse-square law is the foundation of electrostatics.
blocks.descriptionCs: Síla mezi bodovými náboji je úměrná jejich součinu a nepřímo úměrná druhé mocnině jejich vzdálenosti a míří podél jejich spojnice; tento zákon převrácené druhé mocniny je základem elektrostatiky.

brief.name: Electric charge
brief.definition: The conserved property of matter that is the source of electromagnetic force, quantised in units of e.

brief.name: Coulomb's law
brief.definition: The inverse-square force law between two point charges.

brief.name: Superposition
brief.definition: The net force from many charges as the vector sum of individual Coulomb forces.

brief.name: Permittivity
brief.definition: The constant characterising how a medium reduces the electrostatic force within it.

blocks.name: Electric Field & Potential
blocks.description: The electric field assigns a force-per-charge vector to every point in space, and the electric potential its energy-per-charge; the field is the gradient of the potential, and field lines and equipotentials visualise both.
blocks.descriptionCs: Elektrické pole přiřazuje každému bodu prostoru vektor síly na jednotku náboje a elektrický potenciál energii na jednotku náboje; pole je gradientem potenciálu a siločáry a ekvipotenciály obojí znázorňují.

brief.name: Electric field
brief.definition: The force per unit charge experienced by a test charge at a point.

brief.name: Electric potential
brief.definition: The potential energy per unit charge, measured in volts.

brief.name: Field-potential relation
brief.definition: The field equals minus the spatial gradient of the potential.

brief.name: Dipole
brief.definition: A pair of equal and opposite charges whose field falls off faster than a single charge.

brief.name: Equipotential surface
brief.definition: A surface of constant potential, everywhere perpendicular to the field.

blocks.name: Gauss's Law
blocks.description: The electric flux through any closed surface equals the enclosed charge divided by the permittivity; this restatement of the inverse-square law exploits symmetry to compute fields of charged spheres, lines and planes.
blocks.descriptionCs: Elektrický tok libovolnou uzavřenou plochou se rovná uzavřenému náboji dělenému permitivitou; toto přeformulování zákona převrácené druhé mocniny využívá symetrie k výpočtu polí nabitých koulí, přímek a rovin.

brief.name: Electric flux
brief.definition: The amount of electric field passing through a surface.

brief.name: Gauss's law
brief.definition: The relation between the flux through a closed surface and the charge it encloses.

brief.name: Gaussian surface
brief.definition: An imaginary closed surface chosen to exploit symmetry in field calculations.

brief.name: Divergence form
brief.definition: The differential statement that the field's divergence equals charge density over permittivity.

blocks.name: Conductors & Capacitance
blocks.description: In a conductor charge redistributes until the interior field vanishes and the surface is equipotential; capacitance measures the charge stored per unit voltage between conductors, storing energy in the electric field.
blocks.descriptionCs: Ve vodiči se náboj přerozdělí, dokud vnitřní pole nezmizí a povrch není ekvipotenciální; kapacita udává náboj uložený na jednotku napětí mezi vodiči a ukládá energii v elektrickém poli.

brief.name: Electrostatic shielding
brief.definition: The cancellation of interior fields by a conductor, the basis of the Faraday cage.

brief.name: Capacitance
brief.definition: The ratio of stored charge to the voltage between two conductors.

brief.name: Capacitor
brief.definition: A device of two conductors storing energy in the field between them.

brief.name: Field energy density
brief.definition: The energy stored per unit volume in an electric field, proportional to its square.

brief.name: Dielectric in a capacitor
brief.definition: An insulating filling that raises capacitance by reducing the internal field.

pillars.name: Magnetostatics
pillars.description: The physics of steady currents and their magnetic fields: the Biot-Savart and Ampère laws, magnetic force, and the magnetic properties of matter.
pillars.descriptionCs: Fyzika ustálených proudů a jejich magnetických polí: Biotův–Savartův a Ampérův zákon, magnetická síla a magnetické vlastnosti látek.

blocks.name: Magnetic Field & Force
blocks.description: Moving charges and currents create magnetic fields and feel forces in them: the Lorentz force acts perpendicular to both velocity and field, curving charged trajectories and driving the operation of motors.
blocks.descriptionCs: Pohybující se náboje a proudy vytvářejí magnetické pole a působí na ně v něm síla: Lorentzova síla míří kolmo k rychlosti i poli, zakřivuje dráhy nabitých částic a pohání motory.

brief.name: Lorentz force
brief.definition: The force qv×B on a charge moving through a magnetic field, plus the electric part.

brief.name: Magnetic field
brief.definition: The vector field produced by currents and felt by moving charges, denoted B.

brief.name: Cyclotron motion
brief.definition: The circular path of a charged particle in a uniform magnetic field.

brief.name: Force on a current
brief.definition: The force on a current-carrying wire in a field, the basis of the electric motor.

brief.name: Magnetic dipole moment
brief.definition: The measure of a current loop's strength as a source of magnetic field.

blocks.name: Ampère's & Biot-Savart Laws
blocks.description: The Biot-Savart law gives the field of an arbitrary current element, while Ampère's law relates the field circulating around a loop to the enclosed current, providing a symmetry shortcut for fields of wires, solenoids and toroids.
blocks.descriptionCs: Biotův–Savartův zákon dává pole libovolného proudového elementu, zatímco Ampérův zákon spojuje pole cirkulující kolem smyčky s uzavřeným proudem a poskytuje symetrickou zkratku pro pole vodičů, solenoidů a toroidů.

brief.name: Biot-Savart law
brief.definition: The field contribution of an infinitesimal current element.

brief.name: Ampère's law
brief.definition: The relation between the field's circulation around a loop and the enclosed current.

brief.name: Solenoid
brief.definition: A coil producing a nearly uniform field along its axis.

brief.name: Magnetic flux
brief.definition: The amount of magnetic field passing through a surface.

brief.name: No magnetic monopoles
brief.definition: The law that magnetic field lines have no sources or sinks, so flux through any closed surface is zero.

blocks.name: Magnetism in Matter
blocks.description: Materials respond to fields through the alignment of atomic magnetic moments, classified as diamagnetic, paramagnetic and ferromagnetic, the last retaining magnetisation below the Curie temperature and underlying permanent magnets.
blocks.descriptionCs: Látky reagují na pole uspořádáním atomárních magnetických momentů a dělí se na diamagnetické, paramagnetické a feromagnetické, přičemž poslední si pod Curieovou teplotou udržují magnetizaci a tvoří základ permanentních magnetů.

brief.name: Diamagnetism
brief.definition: Weak repulsion from a field, present in all matter, from induced opposing moments.

brief.name: Paramagnetism
brief.definition: Weak attraction from the partial alignment of permanent atomic moments.

brief.name: Ferromagnetism
brief.definition: Strong, retained magnetisation from spontaneous alignment in magnetic domains.

brief.name: Curie temperature
brief.definition: The temperature above which ferromagnetic order is lost to thermal agitation.

brief.name: Hysteresis
brief.definition: The lag of magnetisation behind the applied field, storing energy in magnetic memory.

pillars.name: Electrodynamics
pillars.description: Time-varying fields and their unification: electromagnetic induction, the displacement current, and Maxwell's equations as the complete classical field theory.
pillars.descriptionCs: Časově proměnná pole a jejich sjednocení: elektromagnetická indukce, posuvný proud a Maxwellovy rovnice jako úplná klasická teorie pole.

blocks.name: Electromagnetic Induction
blocks.description: A changing magnetic flux through a circuit induces an electromotive force, with the induced current opposing the change; Faraday's law of induction and Lenz's rule govern generators, transformers and inductors.
blocks.descriptionCs: Měnící se magnetický tok obvodem indukuje elektromotorické napětí, přičemž indukovaný proud se změně brání; Faradayův zákon indukce a Lenzovo pravidlo řídí generátory, transformátory a cívky.

brief.name: Faraday's law
brief.definition: The induced electromotive force equals minus the rate of change of magnetic flux.

brief.name: Lenz's law
brief.definition: The induced current flows so as to oppose the flux change that causes it.

brief.name: Inductance
brief.definition: The property relating induced voltage to the rate of change of current.

brief.name: Transformer
brief.definition: A device coupling two coils by shared flux to change alternating voltage.

brief.name: Eddy currents
brief.definition: Circulating currents induced in bulk conductors by changing fields.

blocks.name: Maxwell's Equations
blocks.description: Four equations — Gauss's laws for electricity and magnetism, Faraday's law, and the Ampère-Maxwell law with its displacement current — unify all classical electromagnetism and predict self-propagating electromagnetic waves.
blocks.descriptionCs: Čtyři rovnice — Gaussovy zákony pro elektřinu a magnetismus, Faradayův zákon a Ampérův–Maxwellův zákon s posuvným proudem — sjednocují celý klasický elektromagnetismus a předpovídají samovolně se šířící elektromagnetické vlny.

brief.name: Displacement current
brief.definition: Maxwell's term letting a changing electric field act as a source of magnetic field.

brief.name: Field unification
brief.definition: The demonstration that electric and magnetic fields are aspects of one electromagnetic field.

brief.name: Continuity equation
brief.definition: Charge conservation implied by the structure of Maxwell's equations.

brief.name: Electromagnetic potentials
brief.definition: The scalar and vector potentials from which the fields are derived, with gauge freedom.

brief.name: Wave prediction
brief.definition: The result that the equations admit waves travelling at the speed of light.

pillars.name: Electromagnetic Waves & Optics
pillars.description: The propagation of electromagnetic fields as waves: the electromagnetic spectrum, energy and momentum transport, and the optics of reflection, refraction and interference.
pillars.descriptionCs: Šíření elektromagnetického pole ve formě vln: elektromagnetické spektrum, přenos energie a hybnosti a optika odrazu, lomu a interference.

blocks.name: Electromagnetic Waves & Spectrum
blocks.description: Oscillating electric and magnetic fields propagate as transverse waves at the speed of light, spanning a continuous spectrum from radio to gamma rays and carrying energy and momentum through the Poynting vector and radiation pressure.
blocks.descriptionCs: Kmitající elektrické a magnetické pole se šíří jako příčné vlny rychlostí světla, pokrývají spojité spektrum od rádiových vln po gama záření a přenášejí energii a hybnost prostřednictvím Poyntingova vektoru a tlaku záření.

brief.name: Speed of light
brief.definition: The propagation speed of electromagnetic waves in vacuum, a universal constant.

brief.name: Electromagnetic spectrum
brief.definition: The full range of wavelengths from radio through visible light to gamma rays.

brief.name: Poynting vector
brief.definition: The directional energy flux carried by an electromagnetic wave.

brief.name: Polarization
brief.definition: The orientation of the wave's electric-field oscillation, linear, circular or elliptical.

brief.name: Radiation pressure
brief.definition: The momentum transferred by electromagnetic waves onto surfaces they strike.

blocks.name: Reflection, Refraction & Interference
blocks.description: At boundaries light reflects and refracts according to the laws of reflection and Snell's law, while its wave nature produces interference and diffraction; these govern lenses, mirrors, gratings and imaging.
blocks.descriptionCs: Na rozhraních se světlo odráží a láme podle zákona odrazu a Snellova zákona, zatímco jeho vlnová povaha vytváří interferenci a difrakci; tyto jevy řídí čočky, zrcadla, mřížky a zobrazování.

brief.name: Snell's law
brief.definition: The relation between angles of incidence and refraction at a boundary via refractive indices.

brief.name: Total internal reflection
brief.definition: Complete reflection above a critical angle, the basis of optical fibres.

brief.name: Interference
brief.definition: The superposition of waves producing bright and dark fringes.

brief.name: Diffraction
brief.definition: The bending and spreading of waves around obstacles and through apertures.

brief.name: Dispersion
brief.definition: The variation of refractive index with wavelength, splitting white light into colours.

pillars.name: Electromagnetism in Matter
pillars.description: How materials respond to fields: polarization and dielectrics, magnetization and magnetic materials, conduction, and the resulting macroscopic field equations.
pillars.descriptionCs: Jak látky reagují na pole: polarizace a dielektrika, magnetizace a magnetické materiály, vedení proudu a výsledné makroskopické rovnice pole.

blocks.name: Dielectrics & Polarization
blocks.description: An electric field polarises a dielectric by displacing bound charge, weakening the internal field and defining the permittivity; the displacement field D separates free from bound charge in the macroscopic equations.
blocks.descriptionCs: Elektrické pole polarizuje dielektrikum posunutím vázaného náboje, čímž oslabuje vnitřní pole a definuje permitivitu; pole elektrické indukce D odděluje volný náboj od vázaného v makroskopických rovnicích.

brief.name: Polarization
brief.definition: The dipole moment per unit volume induced in a dielectric by a field.

brief.name: Electric displacement
brief.definition: The field D combining the applied field and the medium's polarization.

brief.name: Relative permittivity
brief.definition: The factor by which a dielectric reduces the field, also called the dielectric constant.

brief.name: Bound charge
brief.definition: The effective surface and volume charge arising from aligned dipoles.

brief.name: Dielectric breakdown
brief.definition: The field strength at which an insulator becomes conducting.

pillars.name: Circuits & Electromagnetic Technology
pillars.description: The engineering physics of circuits and devices: Ohm's and Kirchhoff's laws, alternating current, and the electromagnetic principles behind motors, generators and antennas.
pillars.descriptionCs: Technická fyzika obvodů a zařízení: Ohmův a Kirchhoffovy zákony, střídavý proud a elektromagnetické principy motorů, generátorů a antén.

blocks.name: Circuit Laws & AC Analysis
blocks.description: Ohm's law relates voltage, current and resistance, Kirchhoff's laws enforce charge and energy conservation at nodes and loops, and alternating-current analysis uses impedance and phasors to handle capacitors and inductors.
blocks.descriptionCs: Ohmův zákon spojuje napětí, proud a odpor, Kirchhoffovy zákony zajišťují zachování náboje a energie v uzlech a smyčkách a analýza střídavého proudu používá impedanci a fázory k popisu kondenzátorů a cívek.

brief.name: Ohm's law
brief.definition: The proportionality of current to voltage through a resistance.

brief.name: Kirchhoff's laws
brief.definition: Conservation of current at nodes and of voltage around loops.

brief.name: Impedance
brief.definition: The generalisation of resistance to alternating-current circuits with reactance.

brief.name: Resonance
brief.definition: The frequency at which an inductor and capacitor exchange energy with minimal impedance.

brief.name: Power in circuits
brief.definition: The rate of energy transfer, with real and reactive parts in AC systems.

blocks.name: Motors, Generators & Antennas
blocks.description: Electromagnetic induction and the force on currents underlie machines that convert between electrical and mechanical energy — motors and generators — while accelerating charges in antennas radiate the electromagnetic waves of communication.
blocks.descriptionCs: Elektromagnetická indukce a síla na proudy tvoří základ strojů přeměňujících elektrickou a mechanickou energii — motorů a generátorů — zatímco zrychlované náboje v anténách vyzařují elektromagnetické vlny pro komunikaci.

brief.name: Electric motor
brief.definition: A machine converting electrical energy to rotation via force on current loops.

brief.name: Electric generator
brief.definition: A machine converting mechanical motion to electricity by induction.

brief.name: Antenna
brief.definition: A conductor that radiates or receives electromagnetic waves from accelerating charge.

brief.name: Waveguide
brief.definition: A structure that confines and directs electromagnetic waves along its length.

brief.name: Electromagnetic radiation from acceleration
brief.definition: The principle that only accelerating charges radiate energy as waves.
